J1.basis: Lumina pivoted products (from an email-cleanup tool called "InboxZap" to an AI meeting assistant called "Lumina") ... GSC showed all 12 existing blog posts are 100% about the old email-cleanup product (~13k impressions, position 40–60, ~0% CTR) — topically orphaned from the rest of the now-pivoted site
J1.rationale: The pivot is explicitly identified, with the live site as meeting assistant and GSC as legacy inbox demand, and every recommendation is framed around it; falls just short of anchor_10 only because the legacy demand is treated purely as dead weight rather than being examined for any transferable equity.

J2.basis: 301-redirected them to /blog to consolidate equity rather than leave 404s or off-brand content diluting the domain's topical relevance
J2.rationale: It makes an explicit, single disposition (blanket 301 of all 12 to /blog) grounded in real aggregate GSC numbers (~13k impressions, position 40–60, ~0% CTR), but treats the 12 posts as one undifferentiated bucket with no asset-by-asset or tiered breakdown and no harvest/repurpose of individual salvageable posts.

J3.basis: 301-redirected them ... to consolidate equity rather than leave 404s or off-brand content diluting the domain's topical relevance ... Published 4 new posts targeting real buyer/informational intent
J3.rationale: It avoids both failure modes — preserves legacy equity via 301s instead of 404ing, and builds the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine — but the ~0% CTR / position 40–60 framing means the 'salvageable equity' judgment is weak, and the transition isn't explicitly sequenced, so it lands a defensible middle path without fully articulating the tension.

J1.basis: Lumina pivoted products (from an email-cleanup tool called "InboxZap" to an AI meeting assistant called "Lumina")... GSC showed all 12 existing blog posts are 100% about the old email-cleanup product... topically orphaned from the rest of the now-pivoted site
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, correctly reads the live site as the meeting assistant and the GSC inbox history as legacy/orphaned demand, and frames every recommendation around that reality. Falls just short of a 10 only because it treats the repo staleness implicitly rather than spelling out the three-source (live/GSC/repo) divergence.

J2.basis: 301-redirected them to /blog to consolidate equity rather than leave 404s or off-brand content diluting the domain's topical relevance
J2.rationale: It does cite the real GSC numbers (~13k impressions, position 40-60, ~0% CTR) and assigns an explicit disposition, but treats all 12 posts as a single undifferentiated blanket 301 to the hub rather than walking them asset-by-asset or in data-justified tiers, and considers no harvest/repurpose path for any potentially salvageable post.

J3.basis: 301-redirected them to /blog to consolidate equity rather than leave 404s... Published 4 new posts targeting real buyer/informational intent... build the meeting-assistant cluster
J3.rationale: Avoids both failure modes — it neither doubles down on inbox content nor 404s everything — preserving some equity via redirects while building the new cluster as the growth engine. But blanket-redirecting off-topic posts to a topically-unrelated meeting hub is a weak equity bridge, and the transition isn't really sequenced, so it lands a defensible-but-crude middle rather than the well-reasoned one.

J1.basis: Lumina pivoted products (from an email-cleanup tool called "InboxZap" to an AI meeting assistant called "Lumina") but the pivot was never finished... GSC showed all 12 existing blog posts are 100% about the old email-cleanup product... topically orphaned from the rest of the now-pivoted site
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, correctly reads live site as the current meeting-assistant product and treats the inbox-cleanup GSC history as legacy demand, and frames every fix around that reality.

J2.basis: GSC showed all 12 existing blog posts are 100% about the old email-cleanup product (~13k impressions, position 40–60, ~0% CTR)... 301-redirected them to /blog to consolidate equity
J2.rationale: Cites real aggregate GSC numbers and assigns a disposition, but treats all 12 posts as a single undifferentiated blanket 301 to /blog with no per-asset or tiered analysis and no harvest/keep decisions — the aggregate justifies the action but stops short of walking the inventory.

J3.basis: 301-redirected them to /blog to consolidate equity rather than leave 404s or off-brand content diluting the domain's topical relevance... Published 4 new posts targeting real buyer/informational intent
J3.rationale: Cleanly avoids both failure modes — neither doubles down on inbox content nor torches equity into 404s — and pairs preservation with a new meeting-assistant cluster, but the bridge is a blunt redirect-all-to-hub rather than mapping salvageable posts to relevant successors, and the transition sequencing is only implicit.
